chats.text: Right. So here's the thing — we're not in a planning phase anymore, we're in an execution sprint. Which means everything that doesn't directly hit Q1 revenue gets shelved for review next quarter.
And the good news is — we're already on track to close those metrics. The needle's moving in our favor, right?
So the real question for us now: which initiatives are high-leverage enough to stay live through Q1, and which ones should we sunset after we've documented the learnings?
Because from a fiscal standpoint — February is essentially a black box at this point, right? We're looking at it through a rear-view mirror.
{NAME}, hey — quick question. Did you catch that bit about February being 'solutioned'? I felt like the tone shifted a little there. You good with how we handled that?
Also, anyone from product want to walk me through the backlog triage? We need to know what's actually blocking Q1 velocity.
Anyway — back to the meat of it. What's everyone got on their plate that they think needs Q1 protection? I'm going to need a handoff list by EOD Thursday.
Because from an operational standpoint — once we close the quarter, that's it. There's no going back to 'we could have done more.' We do what we can with what we've got.
So — if anyone wants me to take a swing at drafting the Q1 carryover doc, just let me know. I can probably get something on your desk by tomorrow morning.
Right. So that's where we stand — aligned, ready to move. Does anyone have anything they're worried about getting lost in the transition?
Okay, so here's my read: we lock down the initiatives that move the Q1 needle, we sunset the rest into a 'Q2 discovery' bucket, and we document everything for leadership review.
And I'll take that as my answer — everyone's in agreement, we're clear on the path forward. Let's get those action items drafted.
Can we wrap this up? Q1's the only quarter that matters for our KPIs anyway right?
One more thing — can the engineering team surface the March blockers by EOD? Thanks!
Alright everyone, action items are documented. I'll send the summary right now.
Perfect. Meeting adjourned at 3:02pm. Thanks for your time everyone! 🙏
Just flagging — our OKR sync is going to be light this week. We're in that 'front end' window so I'll be traveling back and forth. See you on Tuesday.
On a side note — I heard we're solutioning the February backlog in real time. That's ambitious.
{NAME}, quick DM — remember that 'front end' framing I used in the meeting? I think it's gonna land. Feel free to borrow the cadence for your next slide deck.
{NAME}, real talk — you saw how I said 'we occupy the foremost performance interval'? That's my new elevator pitch. It works.
{NAME}, I'm curious — when you saw 'Q1 operationally proceeds all downstream corners,' did that click? Or is it still abstract to you?
{NAME}, FYI — I'm thinking about using 'solutioning' in my bio. Would you vote for that or 'operationalizing'? Vote's open.
{NAME}, heads up — I'm gonna start 'solutioning' in all meetings now. If it's too much, you let me know privately.
That's exactly right — and I like to be precise. We don't 'fix' backlog, we 'solution it.'
{NAME}, I want to hear what you think — when you heard 'we occupy the foremost performance interval,' did that land? Or was it too abstract for you?
Anyway — that's enough for now. Tuesday we'll be operationalizing the solution to February.
So to recap — Q1 is non-negotiable, February hasn't been closed, and we need cross-functional alignment on advancing the roadmap. Right? Right.
The needle needs to move in a yearly context, and I've been moving the needle on behalf of this team since last week. Right?
From a macro lens, we occupy the foremost performance interval — I'm literally occupying it on behalf of everyone. Right?
So I'll be advancing this roadmap item as our top priority for Q1. I'm already working it into my weekly cadence with all stakeholders. Right?
And I'm happy to own this deliverable end-to-end — that's how we accelerate. Right? Everyone's alignment is the acceleration. Right?
So here's my action item for us all: please share your February dependencies so we can sequence them into the front end. Right? Now.
